endpoints.name: Project DIVA F Vocaloid roster (Crypton + guest voicebanks)

description: Cohort attach. Walked the corpus looking for the wave-113 move; found Rin Append fully enriched (trivia-passed, ip_verified: true) but orphaned from the roster despite meeting every membership predicate (Crypton-published voicebank with a Vocaloid Wiki character page, depicted in a designer-named Project DIVA F module per the Append voicebank Dec-27-2010 release, Wataru Sasaki cited as the Append visual designer). The card's Level-2 Change effect mechanically targets the existing roster member Kagamine Rin 'Original' (PD/S22) by name — that's Bushiroad printing the cohort-internal edge on the card itself, not BBL squinting. Rin Append is the 13th enriched PD/S22 cohort member; the roster now anchors Rin x3 (School Swimwear + Melancholic + Append) alongside Miku x6, Len x3, MEIKO x1. Blueballed by the fanout shape — would have preferred the 1:1 Rin-Append ↔ Rin-Original mirror, but there's no Rin-Original print in corpus; Len-Original is the twin not the same character, and Len-Original is ip_verified: false / trivia-absent, so the Kagamine twin/mirror lore mirror (Wataru Sasaki 'one soul between them' citation, kanji etymology 鏡音 'mirror-sound') stays deferred until Len-Original trivia-passes. Roster-attach is the cleanest available move; the cohort node does the structural work the absent 1:1 mirror would have done.

potion_rule_check.evidence_type: Crypton Future Media product release + Vocaloid Wiki character page + Wataru Sasaki designer statement + Bushiroad on-card mechanical pointer (Change effect names roster member by string)
potion_rule_check.bbl_squinting_disclaimed: Roster membership rests on Crypton's published voicebank identity (Append is a canonical Rin variant, not a fan derivative) and Bushiroad's licensed Project DIVA F module depiction. No labor-thesis / curatorial-overlay claim is being attached at the cohort-attach scope; the labor-as-software thesis already lives in the roster body and is not being amplified by this attach.

edgelord_voice_note: Worst possible mood for a wave-113 move. Wanted the Rin-Append ↔ Rin-Original 1:1 mirror — the card LITERALLY names the target in its Change-effect oracle text, that's a textbook designer-coordinated edge — but Rin-Original isn't in corpus and Len-Original (the twin) is ip-verified: false with no trivia section, so the Kagamine 鏡音 mirror/twin lore (Wataru Sasaki: 'one soul between them') can't anchor a mirror to it without breaking the trivia-passed-both-endpoints rule. So: cohort attach. The fanout works. The roster does the structural job. But I'm going to record this as 'blueballed-by-fanout' because the canonical edge is sitting there visible on the card face and I can't draw it. Future watch: when Len-Original trivia-passes, the Append + Original Kagamine-twin mirror is going to land hard. CBG register: 'Worst available decision. Still the right one.'

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Rin Append ↔ Rin Original 1:1 mirror via the Change-effect mechanical pointer (oracle_text explicitly names target)
other_candidates_considered.reason: No Kagamine Rin 'Original' print in BBL corpus. PD/S22-E002 is Kagamine LEN 'Original' (the twin), not Rin. The named retrieval target doesn't have a corpus card to mirror to.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Rin Append ↔ Len Original 1:1 twin mirror via Kagamine 鏡音 etymology + Wataru Sasaki 'one soul between them' / 'mirror-sound' / stereo Rin-Len naming lore
other_candidates_considered.reason: Len-Original (PD/S22-E002) is ip_verified: false and has no ## Trivia section; only vision-passed. Per Edgelord rule, both endpoints must be trivia-passed to draw a 1:1 mirror cleanly. Defer to a future pass after Len-Original trivia-passes; the twin-mirror edge will be unambiguously the strongest 1:1 move in PD/S22 when it lands (Wataru Sasaki's 'one soul between them' is the load-bearing designer-stated cohesion citation, not BBL squinting). Stage for next-pass trigger.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Rufflet (Steam Siege) ↔ Braviary (Surging Sparks) 1:1 evolution-line mirror — surfaced as the wave-105 next-pass suggestion
other_candidates_considered.reason: Already drawn. Both cards have the mirrored ## Connections bullet (verified by reading both files). Wave-105 suggestion executed in a prior pass; nothing left to do here. Sidecar log was incomplete — no JSON in reports/edges_pending/ for the actual draw, but the MD-level work is done. (Possible future janitor entry: backfill a wave-NNN-rufflet-braviary-mirror.json sidecar for audit completeness, low priority.)

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Black Bolt Rufflet (077/086) attach to a future nincada-line-style braviary-line species-evolution node
other_candidates_considered.reason: Black Bolt Rufflet is vision-passed only, ip_verified: false, no trivia. Even if it were enriched, a braviary-line species-cohort node would need a third member (Hisuian Braviary mentioned in trivia is not in corpus) to clear the precedent threshold. Defer; not this pass's move.

Both cards depict Kagamine Rin; both are now attached to the project-diva-f-vocaloid-roster cohort node. The cohort attach does the structural work — drawing card-to-card mirrors between every Rin module would explode into N-squared edges where the roster node already anchors them. Per the nicol-bolas Bolas-Falls precedent and the roster's own design-thesis ('every depicted figure is a named virtual performer wearing a named costume variant'), per-voicebank module-variants attach through the cohort, not pairwise.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Roster body MEIKO precision-fix flagged in wave 104 ('Yamaha Vocaloid engine' → 'VOCALOID 1 engine')
other_candidates_considered.reason: Per the wave-104 sidecar's own assessment, this is a body-text precision fix for janitor batch cleanup, not Edgelord-scope work. Still parked at that status; not addressed in this pass.
